name: Rustic Italian Pasta and Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 14 1/2 ounces Nonfat Veg or Chicken Broth (or Vegetable Broth)
15 ounces Cannellini Beans, Rinse and Drain
15 ounces Red Kidney Beans, Rinse and Drain
recipeInstructions:
This simple meatless dinner can be on the table in less than 45 minutes.
Note: The original recipe used regular vegetable broth but stated you could use chicken broth.
Coat a Dutch oven with cooking spray; add oil and place over medium-high heat. Add onion and next three ingredients (carrot, green bell pepper, garlic); sauté until veggies are crisp-tender. Add vegetable broth and the remaining seven ingredients; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add pasta to the vegetable mixture; cover and cook for an additional 10-15 minutes or until pasta is tender. Ladle soup into individual bowls; top each serving with about 1 tablespoon of cheese.
Yield: 8 (1 1/4 cups) servings
Note: Cannellini beans are white kidney beans. You can use any other small white beans or an additional can of red kidney beans if you don't have cannellini beans in the pantry.
According to the original recipe, per serving:
Calories 244, Fat 4.7g, Carbs 39.3g, Protein 12.1g, Sodium 601mg, Fiber 17%
This was very good. We let it sit for a couple of hours before we served it and it thickened up to a stew consistency.
Entered into MasterCook and tested for you by Reggie & Jeff Dwork (reggie@reggie.com)
